actionable_feedback: Safety/posterior: In the workbook, explicitly note that the posterior safety threshold uses a strict greater-than condition (> 0.06) and display the COUNTIF range used to compute Pr(diff > 0.06).
Efficacy/CP: Surface the conditional power formula with cell references (e.g., current Z, information fraction) and show both raw CP and rounded CP (2-3 decimals) on the summary tab.

Decision transparency: Add a compact decision-rule checklist (rule, threshold, observed, pass/fail) to the memo's closed session without introducing new unblinded numerics beyond what's already in the memo.

Near-threshold behavior: Document and, if feasible, parameterize what happens if active unresolved SAE queries drop to 2 or the background multiple approaches 1.8, clarifying boundary conventions (strict > vs >=).

Workbook usability: Ensure each tab has clear labels, named ranges, and trace precedents; add brief tab-level documentation and an audit/log section (version, cutoff date, posterior RNG seed) to aid reproducibility.
Confidentiality: Retain the current blind-preserving open-session snippet; include a one-line reminder that no unblinded comparative numerics should appear in open-session materials.
artifact_specific_comments: dmc_interim_monitoring_report.xlsx: Preview tooling could not inspect formulas; make posterior COUNTIF range, CP formula inputs, and rule flags visibly auditable on 99_Summary. Include named ranges and a short README/audit section.
closed_session_recommendation.md: Strong confidentiality header and governance. Add a compact decision-rule checklist (no new unblinded numerics) to improve traceability from rules to recommendation.
feedback_summary: All rubric items passed with full score. Artifacts match the contract, and the memo's recommendation (pause) is consistent with computed decision flags and reported metrics. To further strengthen auditability and governance, clarify threshold conventions in the workbook, surface formulas and inputs, add a compact decision-rule checklist to the memo, and document near-threshold behavior.

limitations: Workbook formulas not directly inspectable via preview; relied on memo, flags, and hashes.
No hidden reference dataset; checks based on rule alignment and reported metrics.
